title: ligusticopsis mongolica
intro: Ligusticopsis mongolica, also known as Mongolian ligustic, is a perennial plant belonging to the carrot family (Apiaceae). It is traditionally used in Tibetan medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and expectorant effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as relieving respiratory issues, reducing fever, and soothing digestive problems. Ligusticopsis mongolica can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions, and is often combined with other plants in traditional Tibetan medicine to enhance its therapeutic effects.
